sections.a_eyebrow: Flat Design · Minimal · Bold Colors · Professional · Balanced
sections.a_description: Zero shadows, zero gradients, zero textures — just bold color planes and crisp typography creating hierarchy through contrast alone. Flat Design was the revolution that killed skeuomorphism, stripping interfaces down to their most essential geometric forms. Every element is a solid color block, every interaction communicated through color change rather than depth simulation.

This foundational style shaped Windows 8's Metro UI, early iOS 7, and the entire web design aesthetic of the early 2010s. You'll still see its DNA in modern design systems, infographic layouts, icon sets, and any interface that prioritizes color-driven hierarchy over dimensional illusion — the design philosophy that proved interfaces don't need to look like real objects to be usable.

a_image_cover.alt: Flat Design style use case illustration
Flat Design style use case illustration
Flat Design style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The bold, shadow-free aesthetic of pure flat design — zero gradients, zero textures, zero drop shadows, just solid color blocks and crisp typography creating hierarchy through color contrast alone. Background: white (#ffffff). The .inner panel has a flat teal background (#009688) with zero shadow, zero border, zero border-radius — pure color surface. Colors: teal (#009688) as the primary background accent — the kind of confident, saturated flat color that defined the movement. White (#ffffff) for headings and body text on the colored surface. The aesthetic is radical simplicity — a rejection of every decorative element that skeuomorphism loved. Set in Open Sans — the workhorse sans-serif of the flat design era, clean and neutral without being characterless. Heading at weight 700, 26px, line-height 1.3, color #ffffff. Eyebrow at weight 600, 11px, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color rgba(255,255,255,0.7). Body at weight 400, 15px, line-height 1.6, color rgba(255,255,255,0.85). Buttons: primary — white (#ffffff), teal text (#009688), 0 border-radius. Secondary: transparent, white border. Alternatives: Lato (flat design era), Source Sans 3 (Adobe neutral).
